title: Add CRIU version check for checkpoint and restore
body: As the checkpoint/restore implementation in Podman requires at least CRIU 3.11, this vendors in the Go bindings of CRIU and uses these bindings to check the version of the installed CRIU binary.

comments.body: /ok-to-test
/approve
@adrianreber We just merged something that should fix CI, mind rebasing?

@mheon thanks for the review. I changed the things you pointed out and force pushed.

I now get the following if CRIU is too old:

```
#  podman container checkpoint --keep podman-criu-test 
failed to checkpoint container 91b1f32f7: checkpointing a container requires at least CRIU 31100
```
/retest images
/test images
bot, retest this please
/retest ci/prow/images
/retest images
/test images
/test images
this must be a legit error.
Yes, the vendoring is not correct, need to fix that.
I just saw that CRIU is no longer installed in the new test environment. All checkpoint/restore test cases are now skipped.
/approve
lgtm

Hm. I'll talk to Chris and Brent and see about getting CRIU on our test VMs
in Cirrus

For F28 in PAPR, if the packaged version is sufficient, then an edit here would do it: -> https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/.papr.yml#L112

@cevich can you say where we can add additional packages for cirrus?

@baude currently all package installing happens at image-build time in ``contrib/cirrus/packer/*.sh`` for the various distros.  However, if this is something that you expect to update frequently (more than once a week or so), it may make more sense to install it at runtime via the distro-specific ``case`` blocks in ``contrib/cirrus/setup_environment.sh``.  If neither makes sense, let me know.

CRIU would not need to be updated often. Only if a new feature is required, which Podman needs.

Currently Podman needs CRIU from git (that's what I added to the docker file when travis testing was still done). Once CRIU 3.11 is released (probably in the next few weeks), we can just install the Fedora package (for the Fedora tests), once I have updated the package there.

I would like to see checkpoint/restore testing enabled again in Podman before CRIU 3.11 is released to make sure that checkpoint/restore does not break, that would mean installation from git.

But if you say that this is too much effort we can also wait until CRIU 3.11 is released and is part of Fedora. Whatever works best. Let me know if there is anything I can do to help here.

I'm fine with building from Git - we have a few other dependencies we grab this way (runc, for example, we're building a specified commit there).

This will need changes to the Cirrus and Papr scripts and the Dockerfile to install from source, but I don't think they should be that bad

> (that's what I added to the docker file when travis testing was still done)

Oh, that makes it even easier.  I can just copy-paste what you did for the Dockerfile, [something like this for cirrus.](https://paste.fedoraproject.org/paste/X7aZmU6zAuM8I1fovotVRQ) super easy.

Okay done: #1660

I added it for all distros, but should it be just for Ubuntu?
@cevich thanks, looks perfect. CRIU and podman checkpoint/restore should work on all distros.
@adrianreber excellent, okay I'l carry on with that then, thanks.
/retest
All automated tests are now green (except cirrus ci which still is Pending)
/approve
